comment_text.sn32.3-12:1.1: The numbering in the PTS editions goes awry here.

root_text.sn32.3-12:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn32.3-12:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sītavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti?
root_text.sn32.3-12:1.4: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekacco kāyena sucaritaṁ carati, vācāya sucaritaṁ carati, manasā sucaritaṁ carati.
root_text.sn32.3-12:1.8: ‘aho vatāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sītavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyyan’ti.
root_text.sn32.3-12:1.11: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sītavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn32.3-12:1.12: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sītavalāhakānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti.

translation_text.sn32.3-12:0.3: Ten Discourses On How Giving Helps to Become a Cool Thundercloud God
translation_text.sn32.3-12:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn32.3-12:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the gods of cool thunderclouds?”
translation_text.sn32.3-12:1.4: “Mendicant, it’s when someone does good things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn32.3-12:1.6: ‘The gods of cool thunderclouds are long-lived, beautiful, and very happy.’
translation_text.sn32.3-12:1.8: ‘If only, when my body breaks up, after death, I would be reborn in the company of the gods of the cool thunderclouds!’
translation_text.sn32.3-12:1.11: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the gods of cool thunderclouds.
translation_text.sn32.3-12:1.12: This is the cause, this is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the gods of cool thunderclouds.”
